title: Restrictions on Non-Essential Travel Between the US, Canada and Mexico Extended Again — ELLIS PORTER
content:
The Department of Homeland Security has confirmed that the restrictions on nonessential land border travel between the US, Canada and Mexico have been extended to September 21, 2020.
These travel restrictions have been in place since March 21, 2020.
We continue to work with our Canadian and Mexican partners to slow the spread of #COVID19 . Accordingly, we have agreed to extend the limitation of non-essential travel at our shared land ports of entry through September 21.
